sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check eye positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Eye at rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# The animation changed only 2 cells - probably just timer
# Let me compare before/after
print("\nBefore UP:")
for n in last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  Eye at rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The eyes didn't move. The 5th UP had no effect on the eyes - only the timer changed. So it seems like:
- Each eye can move up to 2 steps (10 pixels) from their starting position
- Left eye started at rows 49-53, moved to 44-48 (1 UP), then 39-43 (2 UPs) - maxed out
- Right eye started at rows 49-53, moved to 44-48 (1 UP) - but couldn't move further
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me re-examine. Initially both eyes were at rows 49-53. After the first UP (action 4), both moved to rows 44-48. After the second UP (first of the batch of 4), left moved to 39-43 but right stayed at 44-48. After the 3rd and 4th UPs, nothing changed.
So the left eye could move up 2 steps (10 pixels total), but the right eye could only move up 1 step (5 pixels). This is asymmetric. Maybe the eyes have different movement ranges based on the shape of the character.
Looking at the character shape, the left eye socket area (rows 39-43) seems to be where the left eye should be, and the right eye socket area (rows 44-48) is where the right eye should be. But these don't look like the "correct" eye positions for a face.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the goal isn't about positioning eyes on a face. Maybe it's about something else entirely. Let me look at the character shape again and think about what the "correct" configuration might be.
Actually, I've been wasting a lot of actions. Let me try a different approach. Let me reset the eyes to their original position and try MOUSE to click on something specific.
Actually, first let me check: can I move the right eye down and the left eye down independently? Let me try DOWN.
